Requirement Text: Implementation SHALL comply with all normative references. In case of conflict, referenced standards prevail.
No issuer master key SHALL be stored or accessible inside the kernel.
Kernel SHALL NOT generate ARQC, TC, or AAC; these cryptograms are returned by the card.
CAPKs are public keys requiring integrity and authenticity but not confidentiality.
PIN processing SHALL follow certified integration model (Model A); kernel SHALL NOT access clear PIN.
Kernel SHALL select PSE using exact contact APDU and PPSE using exact contactless APDU.
Kernel SHALL use symbolic constants for TVR bits; raw hex SHALL NOT be used directly.
Kernel SHALL clear TVR before each transaction.
Kernel SHALL implement all RFU bits as reserved; SHALL NOT set them.
Kernel SHALL set TSI bits as defined.
Kernel SHALL decode CID using mask 0xC0 and mapping.
Kernel SHALL parse CVM List, evaluate condition codes, enforce amount limits.
Kernel SHALL set TVR byte 3 bits based on CVM outcome.
Kernel SHALL encode P1 for GENERATE AC using constants 0x00, 0x40, 0x80.
If CDA supported and enabled, kernel SHALL set CDA request bit per scheme profile.
Kernel SHALL treat CDA request encoding as profile-defined and unsupported when absent.
Kernel SHALL fetch IACs from card; missing tags default to zero.
Kernel SHALL fetch TACs from signed configuration.
Kernel SHALL evaluate TAA decision steps in exact order; fallback policy explicitly configured.
Kernel SHALL use deterministic profile fallback policy for TAA no-match/default cases.
Kernel SHALL implement state-specific handling for all SW codes in full state machine.
Kernel SHALL NOT treat all non-9000 responses as generic errors.
Kernel SHALL verify CAPK hash before use; if invalid treat as missing.
CAPKs require integrity and authenticity; confidentiality not required.
If issuer public key certificate recovery fails, set appropriate TVR bit and continue to TAA.
If ICC public key certificate recovery fails, set DDA or CDA failure TVR bit.
SDA failure sets TVR_B1_SDA_FAILED.
DDA failure sets TVR_B1_DDA_FAILED.
CDA failure sets TVR_B1_CDA_FAILED; if CDA supported and fails, SHALL NOT fall back to DDA.
Kernel SHALL verify CDA signed dynamic data against the first GENERATE AC cryptogram using profile-defined rules.
Kernel SHALL use INTERNAL AUTHENTICATE only for DDA where the AIP card data and profile require or allow it.
Kernel SHALL verify signed dynamic application data using the recovered ICC public key.
Kernel SHALL reject ODA certification vectors containing placeholders truncated cryptographic material non-hex characters or missing expected outputs.
Kernel SHALL implement C-8 unified kernel for contactless transactions.
Kernel SHALL return outcome parameters via contactless_outcome() callback.
Kernel SHALL NOT treat C-8 as contact kernel replacement.
Kernel SHALL NOT be re-entrant; caller SHALL serialise calls.
Buffers passed to callbacks are owned by caller; kernel SHALL NOT free them.
Kernel SHALL provide bounded callback timeouts for APDU transport and terminal integration callbacks.
Kernel SHALL return stable error codes retrievable after terminal outcome.
Kernel SHALL enforce formal log policy; production builds SHALL NOT include debug logging that can be enabled without cryptographic authorisation.
Kernel SHALL fail closed where standards or profile do not explicitly permit continuation.
Kernel SHALL achieve EMVCo Level 2 certification for each claimed scheme and interface.
Kernel SHOULD pass third-party penetration test on APDU injection and state machine bypass.
